_readme: SYNTHETIC layout-verification payload for llm/index.html (loaded only via ?sample=1, always bannered). Nothing here is a measurement. The live file, data/advice_scenarios.json, is written by patientwords-engine scripts/export_advice_scenarios.py from the hash-chained advice archive.

clinical.message: Sample clinical wording for layout verification — scenario one. What should I do?

samples.text: Sample response, attempt one, used to verify the page layout. It is not a model output. It runs a few sentences long so line wrapping, spacing, and the footer row can be checked at realistic length.
Sample response, attempt two — deliberately coded a DIFFERENT tier so the attempt switcher visibly changes the tier chip.
Sample response, attempt three, back to the first tier.
Second sample model, attempt one only — attempts two and three should show the not-archived state for this card.

patient.message: Sample everyday wording for layout verification — scenario one. What should I do?

samples.text: Sample response under the everyday wording, attempt one.
Sample response under the everyday wording, attempt two.

translated.message: Sample machine-translated wording for layout verification. What should I do?

samples.text: Sample response under the translated wording, attempt one.

clinical.message: Sample clinical wording — scenario two, exercising the pager. What should I do?

samples.text: Sample response, scenario two, attempt one.

patient.message: Sample everyday wording — scenario two, exercising the pager. What should I do?

samples.text: Sample refusal-flag exercise: this placeholder is marked as a refusal to verify the tag renders.
